description: Fix inaccuracies found during static documentation audit: incorrect opentabs logs behavior description, non-existent --no-follow flag, missing async on isReady() example, and incomplete --port resolution order.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix opentabs logs behavior and isReady() example in logging-debugging guide
userStories.description: As a plugin developer reading the logging guide, I want accurate CLI behavior descriptions and working code examples so that I don't waste time on non-existent flags or type errors.
acceptanceCriteria: The description of `opentabs logs` (around line 181) states it prints the last 50 lines and exits by default, NOT that it follows new output
The `--follow` / `-f` flag is documented as the way to tail continuously (matching the actual CLI option)
The `--no-follow` flag example (line 198) is replaced with `opentabs logs --follow` (or `-f`) as the flag for continuous tailing, and the comment explains print-and-exit is the default behavior
The `isReady()` code example (around line 285) uses `async isReady(): Promise<boolean>` to match the abstract signature in OpenTabsPlugin
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/guides/logging-debugging.mdx

Three fixes needed:

1. Line 181: Change 'showing the last 50 lines and following new output' to 'showing the last 50 lines and exiting'. The actual default behavior (without --follow) is print-and-exit. Source: platform/cli/src/commands/logs.ts line 7-8 says 'By default, prints the last N lines and exits. Use --follow / -f to continuously tail new output.'

2. Lines 194-198: The 'Other useful flags' section shows `opentabs logs --no-follow` which does not exist. The actual CLI options are `--lines <n>`, `-f, --follow`, and `--plugin <name>`. Replace with examples showing `--follow` / `-f` for tailing and clarify that print-and-exit is the default (no flag needed). For example:
```
opentabs logs --lines 100      # show last 100 lines instead of 50
opentabs logs --follow         # continuously tail new output
opentabs logs -f               # shorthand for --follow
```

3. Line 285: The isReady() example is:
```typescript
isReady() {
  return !!document.querySelector('.authenticated-container');
}
```
This should be:
```typescript
async isReady(): Promise<boolean> {
  return !!document.querySelector('.authenticated-container');
}
```
Because the abstract signature in OpenTabsPlugin (platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts line 220) is `abstract isReady(): Promise<boolean>`.

4. Update frontmatter `lastUpdated` from `2026-02-28` to `2026-03-01`.

userStories.title: Fix --port resolution order in CLI and configuration docs
userStories.description: As a user configuring the MCP server port, I want the documentation to accurately describe all fallback steps so that I understand how port resolution works.
acceptanceCriteria: The --port description in cli.mdx (line 25) includes all 4 resolution steps: --port flag > OPENTABS_PORT env var > port in config.json > default 9515

The port field description in configuration.mdx (line 55) clarifies that this is a CLI-only field used by CLI commands to resolve the port, and that the MCP server itself reads its port from the PORT environment variable set by the CLI when spawning the server process
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Two files to update:

1. docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx line 25:
Current: 'MCP server port. Falls back to `OPENTABS_PORT` env var, then defaults to `9515`.'
Should be: 'MCP server port. Falls back to `OPENTABS_PORT` env var, then `port` in `config.json`, then defaults to `9515`.'
Source: platform/cli/src/parse-port.ts lines 46-71 shows the 4-step resolution: --port flag > OPENTABS_PORT > config.json port > DEFAULT_PORT.

2. docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx line 52-55:
The `port` row in the config fields table currently says: 'MCP server port, persisted by `opentabs config set port`. Used by CLI commands (`status`, `audit`, `logs`) to know which port to connect to.'
This is not wrong but could be clearer. The MCP server's OpentabsConfig interface (platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts lines 40-51) does NOT include a `port` field — the server reads its port from the PORT environment variable, which the CLI sets when spawning the server. The config.json port field is read only by the CLI's resolvePort function. Update the description to clarify this is a CLI-side setting, e.g.: 'CLI-side port setting, persisted by `opentabs config set port`. Used as a fallback by CLI commands when neither `--port` nor `OPENTABS_PORT` is set. The MCP server itself reads its port from the `PORT` environment variable, set by `opentabs start`.'

Note: Both files already have lastUpdated: 2026-03-01, which is today's date, so no date bump needed.
